title: v0.4.2 released
description: vLLM v0.4.2 introduced scheduler improvements and higher throughput defaults for common decoder-only models.

title: v0.5.0 major release
description: vLLM v0.5.0 shipped a major runtime update with expanded model compatibility and upgraded serving ergonomics.

title: OpenAI-compatible tools API improvements
description: vLLM improved tool-calling compatibility in its OpenAI-compatible server, including stricter schema handling and streaming fixes.

title: v0.6.0 released
description: vLLM v0.6.0 added broader multimodal inference support and improved memory management for long-context serving.

title: Disaggregated prefill enters beta
description: vLLM published beta support for disaggregated prefill/decode serving architectures to optimize latency under mixed loads.

title: v0.6.2 patch release
description: vLLM v0.6.2 shipped stability fixes for tensor parallel deployments and improved observability metrics exports.

title: v0.7.0-rc1 published
description: vLLM released v0.7.0-rc1 with upgraded plugin hooks and early distributed serving enhancements for large clusters.

title: v0.7.0 stable release
description: vLLM v0.7.0 reached stable with improved production autoscaling signals, scheduler tuning, and expanded backend support.

title: v0.20.0 released
description: vLLM v0.20.0 shipped DeepSeek V4 support, CUDA 13.0 default wheels, PyTorch 2.11, Python 3.14 support, FlashAttention 4 as the default MLA prefill backend, and TurboQuant 2-bit KV cache.

title: v0.7.1 released
description: vLLM v0.7.1 shipped post-0.7 stability fixes, improved memory fragmentation handling, and scheduler bug fixes for production clusters.

title: v0.20.1 patch release
description: vLLM v0.20.1 focused on DeepSeek V4 stabilization and performance improvements, plus bug fixes for CUDA graph capture, memory-pool behavior, structured-output reasoning parser kwargs, and ROCm Quark W4A8 GPT-OSS arguments.
